taskDescription: Drag the correct neuroanatomical terms into the blanks to complete each paragraph about the medulla oblongata.
textField:
At the pyramidal decussation, *75* percent of corticospinal fibres cross to the opposite side, forming the *lateral corticospinal* tract. The remaining uncrossed fibres descend as the *anterior corticospinal* tract.

In Wallenberg syndrome (lateral medullary syndrome), the artery occluded is the *posterior inferior cerebellar* artery (PICA) and the patient shows ipsilateral *Horner's* syndrome with contralateral loss of pain and temperature. The patient also has ipsilateral cerebellar ataxia and *dysphagia*.

The nucleus *gracilis* carries fine touch and proprioception from the *lower limb*, while the nucleus *cuneatus* carries the same modalities from the upper limb. These nuclei are located in the dorsal columns of the *closed* medulla.

The *medial lemniscus* is formed by internal arcuate fibres that cross at the *sensory* decussation level of the medulla. After crossing, these fibres ascend to the *ventral posterolateral* nucleus of the thalamus.

The medulla contains vital centres for *cardiovascular* and *respiratory* control, including the dorsal respiratory group in the nucleus of the tractus solitarius.

overallFeedback.feedback: Good effort! Review the details you missed.
